name: Dave Grusin
description: American composer, arranger, producer, and pianist
abstract: Robert David Grusin (born June 26, 1934) is an American composer, arranger, producer, jazz pianist, and band leader. He has composed many scores for feature films and television, and has won numerous awards for his soundtrack and record work, including an Academy Award and 10 Grammy Awards.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: I befriended Dave Grusin. I played with him at Donte’s. He played piano with the Howard Roberts Quintet. It was me and Howard and Dave... Dave said why don’t you get Tom, and so I was hired to do an episode of “Dan August”
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Wofford
about_quotes.evidence: I worked for Dave Grusin a lot. Dave Grusin wrote beautiful stuff for some comedy shows we used to do
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Wofford interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Dave Grusin and I, that's where we met and became very close buddies
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I made an album with her and Dave Grusin did the arrangements.

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah well through Allegro and GRP... But we had guys like Mintzer, myself, Grusin, Vince Mendoza did something... So it was everybody connected with GRP basically
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: The drummer was Larry Rosen, who started GRP records. Him and his partner Dave Grusin built that record company up and then sold it for 40 million dollars.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
